notes.content: The Department of English is dedicated to the study of the literatures and cultures of those parts of the world in which English is a primary language. Although committed to no single method or approach, the department requires a knowledge of British, American, and Anglophone literary history and an engagement with a range of methodological approaches that foster intellectual curiosity and critical thinking and encourage its students to be not only expert readers and writers but engaged and ethical citizens. Listed below are the lower division preparation courses for the major. THIS MAJOR IS HIGHLY SELECTIVE and the demand for space significantly exceeds space available. To be considered for this major, you must complete the following preparatory courses by the end of spring before transfer: One course in English Composition (Expository Writing), One course in Critical Reading and Writing (Composition and Literature), One year of Survey of Literature in English: British literature to 1850 Either British literature from 1850 to the present or a survey of American literature STRONGLY RECOMMENDED: Two years of one Foreign Language or a combination of courses in Foreign Language and Foreign Literature in Translation. All courses must be taken for a letter grade. For more information regarding this major and UCLA's transfer selection process, visit https://english.ucla.edu/ and https:// admission.ucla.edu . PLEASE NOTE: the community college course listed below have been approved to satisfy the preparation requirements for this major at UCLA, but they may not be exact equivalents of the UCLA courses listed.
notes.contentHtml:
The Department of English is dedicated to the study of the literatures and cultures of those parts of the world in which English is a primary language. Although committed to no single method or approach, the department requires a knowledge of British, American, and Anglophone literary history and an engagement with a range of methodological approaches that foster intellectual curiosity and critical thinking and encourage its students to be not only expert readers and writers but engaged and ethical citizens.


Listed below are the lower division preparation courses for the major. THIS MAJOR IS HIGHLY SELECTIVE and the demand for space significantly exceeds space available.


To be considered for this major, you must complete the following preparatory courses by the end of spring before transfer:

  • One course in English Composition (Expository Writing),
  • One course in Critical Reading and Writing (Composition and Literature),
  • One year of Survey of Literature in English:
  • British literature to 1850
  • Either British literature from 1850 to the present or a survey of American literature

STRONGLY RECOMMENDED: Two years of one Foreign Language or a combination of courses in Foreign Language and Foreign Literature in Translation.

All courses must be taken for a letter grade.


For more information regarding this major and UCLA's transfer selection process, visit https://english.ucla.edu/ and https://admission.ucla.edu.


PLEASE NOTE: the community college course listed below have been approved to satisfy the preparation requirements for this major at UCLA, but they may not be exact equivalents of the UCLA courses listed.

notes.content: ADDITIONAL DEPARTMENTAL REQUIREMENT IN FOREIGN LANGUAGE OR LITERATURE IN TRANSLATION

All English majors MUST complete two years of one foreign language or a combination of courses in foreign language and foreign literature in translation in order to graduate from UCLA. These courses may be completed after transfer, although completing some or all of this requirement prior to transfer is strongly recommended. PLEASE NOTE: Transfer students who have satisfied the UCLA College (Letters and Science) foreign language requirement at the high school level through the IGETC program may satisfy the departmental foreign language requirement with 5 foreign literature in translation courses.
notes.contentHtml:
All English majors MUST complete two years of one foreign language or a combination of courses in foreign language and foreign literature in translation in order to graduate from UCLA. These courses may be completed after transfer, although completing some or all of this requirement prior to transfer is strongly recommended.


PLEASE NOTE: Transfer students who have satisfied the UCLA College (Letters and Science) foreign language requirement at the high school level through the IGETC program may satisfy the departmental foreign language requirement with 5 foreign literature in translation courses.

notes.content: The AP Exam Scores listed below may be used to satisfy the major prep: A score of 4 or higher on the AP English Literature or Language exam will satisfy the requirement for ENGCOMP 3. The IB Exam Scores listed below may be used to satisfy the major prep: A score of 5 or higher on the IB HL English Literature or Language exam will satisfy the requirement for ENGCOMP 3.
notes.contentHtml:
The AP Exam Scores listed below may be used to satisfy the major prep:

  • A score of 4 or higher on the AP English Literature or Language exam will satisfy the requirement for ENGCOMP 3.


The IB Exam Scores listed below may be used to satisfy the major prep:

  • A score of 5 or higher on the IB HL English Literature or Language exam will satisfy the requirement for ENGCOMP 3.
